title: Spaces — named, rule-based scoped dashboards (subsumes tags)
description: Approved plan (plans/radiant-stargazing-sparkle.md). Named scopes (any axis, exact/regex rules); each = a sidebar tab + a scoped Overview. Reuse curation threading: MemberSets + inclusionPredicate (mirror exclusionPredicate) spliced via injectAfter into the existing aggregations. spaces + space_rules tables (00012). Space/rule CRUD + /affected preview. FE: useSpaces, dynamic Spaces sidebar group, /app/space/:id scoped Overview, SpaceRuleForm. Tags are UNUSED -> remove the tag scope path + TagFilter (no migration).
close_reason: Spaces implemented end-to-end (backend inclusion-scope + CRUD/preview, FE sidebar group + scoped Overview + SpaceRuleForm, tags removed). All gates green: go build/vet/task test, FE tsc/build/93 vitest. Live-verified on real data: Work space scoped 3501h→313h, preview matched 18, empty-space=0, no leak.

title: Durable resumable import jobs + live-logs UI (WS)
description: Any user can start import jobs shown in UI: live logs, historical+current runs, what was imported, errors. Page reload rebinds to running job via durable WS (snapshot-on-connect from DB + live hub). UI can start/cancel. Imports idempotent (heartbeat upsert dedup). BE: jobs schema+logs, cancellable worker, REST (list/get/cancel) + WS. FE: import page/modal with live terminal, history, reconnect.
close_reason: Implemented and verified end-to-end via Playwright against real imported data

See commits c039609..328dbc5.

title: Stack Projects 'Total activity' by language (per-project)
description: The Project-detail 'Total activity' chart on the Projects page is single-series flat bars — make it stacked like the Overview one, but by LANGUAGE (the meaningful per-project dimension; matches the Language pie). Overview could stack for free (per-day-per-category existed via the streamgraph); the project payload does NOT expose per-day-per-language. Backend: GetProjectStats groups by day+language already — surface a top-N-languages-per-day matrix ('languagesDaily': aligned to dailyTotal's day axis, top-N + Other) in ProjectStatistics (shape it in internal/stats/stats.go). FE: in Projects.tsx build language ColumnSeries colored by the SAME scale as the Language pie, pass to ColumnChart's stacked 'series' (already supported); keep weekly bucketing. Verify per-day totals still equal dailyTotal.
close_reason: Closed

title: Edit existing name-remapping rules in place (Settings + explorer modal)
description: The listed Name-remapping rules on Settings (and the 'Rename project' explorer modal) are currently view + delete (× ) only. Add EDIT: clicking a rule row (the > chevron) expands an inline, pre-filled RemappingForm (axis + match strategy + pattern + target) to change and Save. Reuse RemappingForm with preset props + an 'edit' mode. Backend: editing the TARGET only is already an upsert (CreateCurationRule dedups on (sender,axis,action,match_type,match_value) and updates new_value); editing the PATTERN or match_type changes rule identity, so implement edit as delete-old + create-new in one action (or add PATCH /curation/:id). Stays reversible/non-destructive (raw records untouched). NOTE: shares files (Settings.tsx, RemappingForm.tsx) with gaka-r8c — do AFTER that lands to avoid edit conflicts.
design: UX: add a little EDIT (pencil) icon on each rule row, next to the × delete. Clicking the pencil opens the inline pre-filled RemappingForm (axis + strategy + pattern + target) to change & Save; × still deletes. (The > chevron can keep showing affected-values detail, or be replaced — but the edit affordance is a dedicated pencil icon, not the chevron.) Reuse RemappingForm with preset props + an edit mode. Backend: target-only edit is an upsert via CreateCurationRule; pattern/match_type change = delete-old + create-new in one action (or PATCH /curation/:id). Non-destructive/reversible. Shares Settings.tsx + RemappingForm.tsx with gaka-r8c — do AFTER it lands.
close_reason: Pencil-edit remapping rules inline; upsert vs delete+create by identity change; tsc/build/104 vitest green.

title: Axis-value inputs use autocomplete (Combobox) for exact matches
description: In SpaceRuleForm (spaces mgmt) and RemappingForm (curation rename/merge), the exact-match value/pattern input should be an autocomplete of the selected axis's real values — reuse the existing ui/combobox.tsx + useAxisValues(axis) hook already used by Settings' hidden-projects/sources comboboxes. Keep regex/template modes as free-text Input (they're patterns, not values); swap to the Combobox only in 'exact' mode. Applies anywhere this axis+value pattern appears.
close_reason: Autocomplete axis values (exact->combobox w/ counts) + live per-strategy preview in RemappingForm (Settings + explorer modal) & SpaceRuleForm; Total activity stacked by category. tsc/build/102 vitest green; live-verified.

title: Add reusable Google Analytics (gtag.js) module to web frontend
description: Add opt-in GA4 tracking: framework-agnostic analytics core (lazy gtag.js loader, pageview/event helpers) gated on VITE_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID, plus a react-router tracker component for SPA page views. Designed to be copy-paste reusable across apps.
close_reason: GA4 module, router tracker, env typing, .env.example added; typecheck/lint/tests/build green

title: Document the query engine subcomponent (docs/QUERY_ENGINE.md)
description: Write an extensive standalone doc for gakatime's query engine — the core subcomponent that turns raw heartbeats into dashboard stats. Cover: the attributed-duration model (gap_seconds computed at ingest, RecomputeGaps, windowless conditional SUM), the hb_rollup_daily fast-path + when it falls back to raw, the curation/scoping layer (HiddenSets/RenameSets/MemberSets; exclusionPredicate / inclusionPredicate / remapExpr / regroupStatRows; the injectAfter range-anchor splice mechanism; the cols maps; rollup-axis gating), the ~11 aggregation functions + their embedded .sql, top-N+Other and weekly bucketing for payload bounding, and the per-owner response cache + invalidation. Include SQL/Go snippets, a request→SQL flow diagram, and an extension guide (how to add a new scoped aggregation). Audience: a new contributor. Output: docs/QUERY_ENGINE.md, linked from docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.
close_reason: docs/QUERY_ENGINE.md written (737 lines, grounded in real code) + linked from ARCHITECTURE.md

title: DB observability: query logging + plan/N+1 detection wrapper
description: Wrap pgx pool operations to log every query (SQL, args, duration) specially, with dev query-plan (EXPLAIN) hooks and per-request query-count to surface N+1 patterns. Go options: pgx v5 QueryTracer interface + github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/tracelog (structured slog logging); github.com/exaring/otelpgx for OpenTelemetry spans; a request-scoped counter middleware to flag N+1 (>threshold queries per request). Evaluate + wire into internal/db pool setup + logging.
design:
RECOMMENDATION: Attach a pgx v5 QueryTracer at pool-config time. This is the single transparent choke-point — zero call-site changes. Every d.Pool.Query/QueryRow/Exec (and tx.Exec) already flows through the pgx tracer hooks, and all call sites already pass a request-scoped ctx (handlers use c.Request().Context()), so log + N+1 + EXPLAIN all attach here for free.

INTEGRATION POINT (our code): internal/db/db.go, func New(ctx, url). Today it calls pgxpool.New(ctx, url) with NO config. Change to:
  cfg, err := pgxpool.ParseConfig(url)
  cfg.ConnConfig.Tracer = <our tracer>
  pool, err := pgxpool.NewWithConfig(ctx, cfg)
The DB struct exposes Pool *pgxpool.Pool as a PUBLIC field used verbatim across sessions.go/curation.go/etc. — we do NOT wrap or replace it, we only enrich its ConnConfig. Add an optional cfg/opts param to db.New (or a db.NewWithObservability) so cmd/gakatime/main.go can pass env-driven settings; tests (testutil.go, main_test.go) keep the plain path.

TRACER COMPOSITION: use github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/multitracer.New(...) to combine three QueryTracers on the one ConnConfig.Tracer slot:
  1. tracelog.TraceLog{Logger: slogAdapter, LogLevel: ...}  -> structured query logging (SQL, args, duration, commandTag, pid) via our existing log/slog default logger.
  2. n1Tracer (custom, ~40 lines) -> request-scoped query counter for N+1 detection.
  3. planTracer (custom, dev-only) -> auto-EXPLAIN on slow SELECTs.
multitracer.New auto-splits by interface, so tracelog also picks up Batch/Connect/CopyFrom/Prepare/Acquire/Release for free.

slog ADAPTER (grounds in our logging.go default slog): tracelog.Logger is one method: Log(ctx, level tracelog.LogLevel, msg string, data map[string]any). Adapt with tracelog.LoggerFunc:
  var slogAdapter = tracelog.LoggerFunc(func(ctx, lvl, msg, data){
     attrs := redact(data)            // drop/mask sensitive args
     slog.Default().Log(ctx, mapLevel(lvl), msg, attrs...)
  })
mapLevel: tracelog LogLevelError->slog.Error, Warn->Warn, Info->Info, Debug/Trace->Debug.

N+1 COUNTER (threads through ctx we already have): middleware (echo) stashes a *reqStats{count int; byNorm map[string]int} via context.WithValue at request start. TraceQueryStart increments count and a normalized-SQL bucket (strip literals/whitespace, collapse IN-lists); at request end the middleware logs WARN if count>threshold or any single normalized statement ran >nearIdenticalThreshold times (classic N+1: same statement in a loop). pgx passes the ctx from TraceQueryStart through to TraceQueryEnd, and our handlers already pass the request ctx into d.Pool.Query, so no signature changes.

DEV PLAN CAPTURE (planTracer): in TraceQueryStart stash the SQL+args+start-time in the returned ctx; in TraceQueryEnd, if dev-flag set AND duration>threshold AND statement is a read (SQL starts with SELECT/WITH, case-insensitive, after trimming) -> run `EXPLAIN (FORMAT TEXT) <sql>` (NOT EXPLAIN ANALYZE, and NEVER on writes) on a FRESH pool conn with the SAME args, log plan at WARN. Must guard against recursion (the EXPLAIN query itself fires the tracer — tag its ctx with a skip flag) and never run inside the caller`s tx.

GOTCHAS (call out in impl):
 - REDACTION IS MANDATORY. Token/password/salt values flow as $-args into d.Pool: sessions.go GetUserByToken/GetUserByRefreshToken/InsertAPIToken/CreateAccessTokens/DeleteTokens (token, refresh_token) and users (hashed_password, salt_used). Raw arg logging leaks them. Default to LOGGING ARGS ONLY IN DEV, and even then redact by matching the SQL (tables auth_tokens/refresh_tokens/users or columns token/password/salt) -> replace those positional args with "***". Safest default: log arg COUNT + types in prod, full args only in dev after redaction.
 - Tracer fires for EVERY query incl. migrations, Ping, pool warmup, and the internal EXPLAIN — filter/skip these (skip flag in ctx; migrations run via a separate path in migrate.go so mostly N/A for the pool).
 - EXPLAIN ANALYZE actually executes -> forbidden for writes; we use plain EXPLAIN and gate to reads only.
 - N+1 counter needs the middleware installed on the echo server (internal/server) so ctx carries reqStats; queries outside an HTTP request (import worker, CLI create-user) simply have no counter in ctx -> no-op, which is fine.
notes:
LIBRARY OPTIONS + CITATIONS (pgx v5 v5.10.0 already in go.mod):

1. pgx v5 QueryTracer (native hook — the choke-point). Interface:
   TraceQueryStart(ctx, *pgx.Conn, TraceQueryStartData{SQL string; Args []any}) context.Context
   TraceQueryEnd(ctx, *pgx.Conn, TraceQueryEndData{CommandTag pgconn.CommandTag; Err error})
   ctx returned by Start is passed to End -> stash start-time/req-state there.
   src: https://github.com/jackc/pgx/blob/master/tracer.go ; docs: https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/jackc/pgx/v5

2. github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/tracelog (SHIPS WITH pgx — no new dep) -> structured logging.
   TraceLog{Logger; LogLevel; Config} implements QueryTracer + Batch/Connect/CopyFrom/Prepare/Acquire/Release tracers.
   Logger iface: Log(ctx, level LogLevel, msg string, data map[string]any). Adapter type LoggerFunc.
   LogLevels: Trace=6,Debug=5,Info=4,Warn=3,Error=2,None=1. Logs SQL, args, time/duration, commandTag, pid.
   -> wrap our log/slog default via tracelog.LoggerFunc. docs: https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/tracelog

3. github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/multitracer (SHIPS WITH pgx) -> combine tracers on one ConnConfig.Tracer.
   New(tracers ...pgx.QueryTracer) *Tracer, auto-splits by interface. Lets tracelog + custom N+1 + custom EXPLAIN (+ otelpgx later) coexist. docs: https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/multitracer

4. github.com/exaring/otelpgx (OPTIONAL, future) -> OTel spans per query; same hook: cfg.ConnConfig.Tracer = otelpgx.NewTracer(); pgxpool.NewWithConfig(...). Also RecordStats(pool) for pool metrics. Requires recent Go. repo: https://github.com/exaring/otelpgx ; docs: https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/exaring/otelpgx ; alt: https://github.com/pgx-contrib/pgxotel
   Decision: NOT needed for phase 1 (no OTel collector wired yet). Defer; the multitracer slot makes adding it trivial later.

5. N+1 detection: no dedicated Go lib — standard pattern is a custom QueryTracer + request-scoped counter in context (increment in TraceQueryStart, evaluate at request end, WARN on count>N or same-normalized-SQL>M). Confirmed viable because our handlers already thread c.Request().Context() into every d.Pool call. ref/background: https://github.com/jackc/pgx/discussions/1677 , https://github.com/jackc/pgx/issues/2021

6. EXPLAIN capture: also custom (no built-in). Re-run plain EXPLAIN (never ANALYZE, never on writes) on a fresh conn in TraceQueryEnd when dev-flag set and duration>threshold.

ENV FLAGS (follow config.go HAKA_* + getEnvBool/getEnvInt convention; gate off cfg.Env=="dev"/IsDev()):
  HAKA_DB_LOG_QUERIES   (bool, default false in prod / on in dev) — enable tracelog query logging.
  HAKA_DB_LOG_ARGS      (bool, default false) — log (redacted) args; dev-only recommended.
  HAKA_DB_N1_THRESHOLD  (int, default 20) — queries/request to WARN.
  HAKA_DB_N1_DUP_THRESHOLD (int, default 10) — identical normalized statements/request.
  HAKA_DB_EXPLAIN_SLOW_MS (int, default 0=off; dev-only) — auto-EXPLAIN reads slower than this.

PHASED BREAKDOWN (ship incrementally; each phase independently valuable):
  Phase 1 — Query logging: switch db.New to ParseConfig+NewWithConfig; add slog->tracelog.LoggerFunc adapter; attach tracelog.TraceLog via multitracer; implement redaction (mask token/password/salt args by SQL match); wire HAKA_DB_LOG_QUERIES / HAKA_DB_LOG_ARGS. Verify migrations/Ping noise is acceptable/filtered.
  Phase 2 — N+1 counter: echo middleware stashes reqStats in ctx; custom n1Tracer increments count + normalized-SQL buckets in TraceQueryStart; middleware logs WARN at request end on threshold; HAKA_DB_N1_THRESHOLD / HAKA_DB_N1_DUP_THRESHOLD.
  Phase 3 — Dev plan capture: custom planTracer; on slow read in TraceQueryEnd, run EXPLAIN on fresh conn with recursion-skip flag; reads-only guard; HAKA_DB_EXPLAIN_SLOW_MS.
  Phase 4 (optional/future) — otelpgx into the same multitracer once an OTel pipeline exists.

RISKS: sensitive-arg leakage (mitigated by mandatory redaction + args-off-by-default in prod); tracer overhead on hot heartbeat-ingest path (keep logging at Debug, N+1 counter O(1) map ops); EXPLAIN re-run cost/recursion (dev-only + skip flag + reads-only); noise from non-request queries (import worker/CLI have no reqStats ctx -> N+1 no-ops safely).
close_reason: Implemented + merged; go build/vet/task test + FE tsc/102 vitest all green. b23 transparent tracer (off by default, redaction); saq /sources/health + panel. Also fixed .gitignore hiding cmd/gakatime/main.go.

comments.author: DJ Daniels
comments.text: Implemented all 3 phases (query logging + redaction, N+1 counter, dev EXPLAIN) as transparent pgx v5 QueryTracer via multitracer — no new deps, no call-site changes, DB.Pool stays public. New files: internal/db/observability.go (+_test.go), internal/server/n1.go. Surgical edits: config.go (HAKA_DB_* keys), server.go (n1 middleware reg), main.go (NewWithObservability wiring). All 8 observability tests pass; go build/vet clean. NOTE: unrelated test failure TestSourceHealthShape in the concurrent agent's sources_health_test.go (dup-key on unique_heartbeats) — not caused by this change. Not committing/pushing per coordinator review.

title: Heartbeats: plugin/source check-in health panel
description: On the Heartbeats page, a section listing every ingestion source AFTER the wakatime import completes (all sources imported). For each source show its LAST CHECK-IN time (relative, e.g. '2h ago' / '3 days ago') and a status state — active / idle / STALE / silent — so you can tell at a glance when a wakatime plugin has stopped reporting. Sources = editor/plugin/machine (the thing sending heartbeats). Data: MAX(time_sent) per source — reuse GET /heartbeats/group?groupBy=editor|plugin|machine (returns value,count,firstSeen,lastSeen) or a small dedicated /sources/health endpoint. Context: editor plugins point ~/.wakatime.cfg api_url at gakatime and ingest directly, so this is THE ingestion-health / 'is my tracking still working' view.
close_reason: Implemented + merged; go build/vet/task test + FE tsc/102 vitest all green. b23 transparent tracer (off by default, redaction); saq /sources/health + panel. Also fixed .gitignore hiding cmd/gakatime/main.go.

title: Projects aggregate rail: cross-project active-files table (lynchpins)
description: On the Projects 'Across all projects' rail, add a small table of the most active FILES across ALL projects, with the count of DISTINCT projects each file appears in — to surface shared interfaces/lynchpins that span projects. Backend: new aggregate endpoint (top ty='file' entities across the owner's heartbeats: entity, attributed seconds, distinct-project count), curation/hide aware, ordered by project-count (lynchpins) then time. Frontend: compact table in the aggregate rail.
close_reason: Cross-project /files endpoint (lynchpins-first, curation-aware) + Projects-rail table with projects>1 highlighting. Verified live: catalyst-operator App.tsx/client.ts/main.py surface as real cross-project shared interfaces.

title: Remove strangler-fig ApexCharts code + renderer switcher (go D3-only)
description:
The Apex-to-D3 strangler-fig migration is complete — D3 is the chosen renderer. Remove the now-dead Apex half and the switcher:
- Delete the *Apex.tsx chart impls (Column/Pie/Heatmap/Radar/HourBar/FileBar/Timeline).
- Collapse each <Name>.tsx switcher to render the D3 impl directly (or rename <Name>D3 -> <Name>); drop useRenderer branching.
- Remove the strangler-fig React pieces: viz/RendererProvider.tsx, viz/RendererToggle.tsx, viz/rendererContext.ts, the Apex|D3 topbar toggle in AppShell, and the RendererProvider wrapper in main.tsx.
- Remove apexcharts + react-apexcharts from web/package.json; drop apexTheme/base Apex config; update any imports/tests.
Acceptance: tsc/lint/build + vitest green; every chart still renders (D3); no dead Apex refs. NOTE: do AFTER the in-flight capture-groups + synthwave theme work lands (both touch web/ charts/config).
close_reason: Apex removed, D3-only. 19 files deleted, ~725 net src lines removed. tsc/build/102 vitest/lint green.

title: Synthwave/cyberpunk dark theme (first pass)
description: Retune the DARK theme to a synthwave/cyberpunk aesthetic: deep purple/black background, neon magenta+cyan accents, subtle neon glow on primary/active/focus, a synthwave chart palette (CHART_COLORS), tasteful grid/gradient atmosphere. Keep legibility/contrast. Files: web/src/index.css (@theme + .dark tokens), lib/config.ts palette, theme provider if needed. First pass — iterate after.
close_reason: Implemented + verified live: capture/replace-group template remaps (^@(.*)-> \1 strips @-projects), Explorer badge for regex/template rows, and the synthwave dark theme. Commits for capture-groups + badge + synthwave.

title: Explorer: show remap badge for regex/template rules (not just exact)
description: HeartbeatExplorerTable getRenamedTo only matches EXACT rename rules (axis+value literal lookup), so rows affected by a REGEX remapping (e.g. ^Meet - -> Meeting) show no '-> Meeting' badge, though they are remapped in dashboards. Exact renames + the Hidden badge work. Fix (FE-only): after the exact-map check, iterate the axis's regex rename rules and test new RegExp(pattern).test(value) -> show newValue; for template rules compute value.replace(regex,template). Bundle with gaka-9zw (capture groups) — same badge code path. Blocked on Phase 1 test-framework (a922 owns web/).
close_reason: Implemented + verified live: capture/replace-group template remaps (^@(.*)-> \1 strips @-projects), Explorer badge for regex/template rows, and the synthwave dark theme. Commits for capture-groups + badge + synthwave.

title: Favorite projects + 'favorites only' filter
description: Star projects to reduce noise. Storage: curation action 'favorite' (axis=project, matchValue=name) — reuses curation CRUD + useCuration. FE: star toggle on Explorer rows + Projects selector + top-projects bar; favorites pinned to top of selector; a '★ Favorites only' toolbar toggle. MVP = FE-only client filter of the project list/selector. Full = favoritesOnly=true param adds inclusion predicate (AND project = ANY($favs)) threaded through the aggregation paths like hide (project axis). Phased: MVP then server-side filter.
notes: PARKED / needs design before implementing. 'Favoriting projects' is under-specified — decide what it actually DOES: just a 'favorites only' dashboard filter? pin/reorder favorites to the top of selectors? a dedicated favorites view/landing filter? scope the real behavior first.

title: Regex remap capture/replace groups (template match_type)
description: Extend rename remappings with capture groups: add match_type 'template' that emits regexp_replace(col, $pattern, $template) in remapExpr (Postgres backrefs \1,\2), e.g. ^@(.*)$ -> \1 turns @swarm-graph into swarm-graph. No migration (match_type is text). Validate template (guarded regexp_replace -> 400). /affected returns value->mappedTo. FE: RemappingForm capture mode + live preview. Reuses the query-time remap plumbing (all aggregations, reversible, audit-preserved) — low effort.
close_reason: Implemented + verified live: capture/replace-group template remaps (^@(.*)-> \1 strips @-projects), Explorer badge for regex/template rows, and the synthwave dark theme. Commits for capture-groups + badge + synthwave.

title: Implement the parked 2-layer test pyramid (unit + e2e)
description: Matrices in docs/testing/TEST_MATRIX.md (backend unit; frontend Vitest+RTL+msw; Playwright e2e). Enabling refactors: extract FE bucketing/chart-mapping to pure helpers; add data-testid + data-chart-ready hooks. Isolated gakatime_test DB + TestMain already exist to build on.
notes: Phase 1 DONE + committed (33c46b1 backend AIO harness+DRY+P0 gaps; aa566b8 frontend Vitest 70 tests). Remaining: Phase 2 = Playwright e2e suite + data-testid/data-chart-ready hooks + P0 smoke path.

title: HTTP handler-level integration test harness
description: internal/handler has no DB-backed test harness (its tests are pure httptest). Add a TestMain that provisions/migrates the isolated gakatime_test DB + auth-token/cache/worker wiring, to enable true HTTP e2e tests (e.g. POST /curation then GET /stats,/projects,/momentum asserting merged JSON). Current regex-remap coverage is DB-function-level (TestRegexRemapAcrossAllAggregations).
close_reason: Handler HTTP integration harness landed in internal/testutil (TestMain + NewHarness/MintUser/Router) with integration_test.go driving real HTTP: stats/rollup, curation hide/rename + /affected, regex remap across /stats+/projects, project-detail-by-display, full auth cycle. Commit 33c46b1.

title: Heartbeats Explorer page (multi-level group-by any axis, v1 view/audit)
description: New /app/heartbeats page: group heartbeats by any whitelisted label axis, multi-level nesting (e.g. language->project, language->source). Group by source too (editor/plugin/machine/platform/user_agent). Collapsible lazy tree, leaf=paginated rows, per-row JSON drawer, date-range+entity filters, Table/JSON toggle. Backend: single-level group endpoint (groupBy col + equality filters, whitelisted) + paginated rows endpoint. v1 read-only; v2 (later)=remap/rename a group's label across its heartbeats.
close_reason: Implemented and verified end-to-end via Playwright against real imported data

title: Dark mode: system-aware toggle + persisted preference
description: Proper dark mode for the React dashboard: ThemeProvider + useTheme hook, toggle in the topbar (sun/moon), persists to localStorage, respects prefers-color-scheme by default, applies .dark class, and re-themes ApexCharts (dark palette) reactively. Tailwind v4 dark tokens likely already partly in index.css. QUEUED to run AFTER the import-UI FE agent finishes to avoid web/ file conflicts.
close_reason: Implemented and verified end-to-end via Playwright against real imported data

title: Space dashboards always take the raw path (rollup-with-space fast path unused)
description: Found while documenting the query engine (gaka-bj4). The Stats handler gates the rollup fast-path on !spaceRequested, so ANY ?space= request skips hb_rollup_daily and scans raw — even when the Space's rules only target rollup-stored axes (project/language/editor/platform/machine). MemberSets.HasMemberOutside(RollupAxes) exists + is tested as the mirror of HasHiddenOutside but is effectively dead in the live path. Options: (a) enable the rollup fast-path for space scopes whose rules are all rollup axes (use HasMemberOutside to decide), making scoped 'All time' as fast as unscoped; or (b) if intentional belt-and-braces, document why. Perf opportunity now that Spaces are a primary surface.

title: Overview 'Total activity': offer a stacked bar (by category or project)
description: The Total-activity column chart shows a single total per bucket, not a stacked breakdown. The Category streamgraph covers 'what kind of work over time', but a stacked bar (by category, or by top projects) on Total activity would show composition per day/week. Decide: stack by category or by project? Small chart change.
design: Decision: stack by CATEGORY (Browsing/Coding/AI-Coding/Meeting/…) — the natural 'what kind of work' breakdown, and the data already exists (GetCategoryDaily powers the category streamgraph). Feed that per-day-per-category data into the Overview 'Total activity' chart and render it as a STACKED column chart (currently a single-series flat bar). Reuse the category color scale used by the streamgraph/category breakdown. Project-stacking can be a later toggle.
close_reason: Autocomplete axis values (exact->combobox w/ counts) + live per-strategy preview in RemappingForm (Settings + explorer modal) & SpaceRuleForm; Total activity stacked by category. tsc/build/102 vitest green; live-verified.

title: Embeddable profile stats widget (HTML/Markdown link)
description: A shareable stats card/badge you can link or embed in a GitHub profile README, blog, or site — Wakatime-'weekly stats'-style. Likely an endpoint that renders an SVG (top languages/projects/editors + total time for a range) plus a copyable markdown/HTML snippet, extending the existing /badge/svg mechanism. Public (uuid-scoped) like badges; theme-able. IMPLEMENT AFTER mainline (test pyramid e2e + capture-groups + favorites).
design: Widget library side panel (discovery/surfacing UX). Each dashboard page gets a 'Widgets' button in the toolbar. Clicking it opens a RIGHT-HAND SIDE PANEL (~40% of viewport width) — a scannable list/library of the available embeddable widgets. Each list entry shows: a small live preview/thumbnail of the widget, its title, and its copyable link/embed URLs in each format (shields.io badge URL, HTML /iframe snippet, Markdown snippet) with per-format one-click copy. Context-aware: the panel surfaces widgets relevant to the current page/scope — e.g. on a Space page it offers widgets scoped to that Space; on a project-detail page, project-scoped widgets; on Overview, the account-wide set. This panel is the FRONT DOOR for the embeddable-widget feature — the SVG/badge endpoints in the description are the backend that produces each widget; this side panel is how a user discovers and grabs the links. Implementation sketch: slide-over from the right (shadcn/Radix Sheet, ~40vw, dismissible); list items are cards (preview + title + copy-buttons per format). The 'Widgets' trigger lives in the shared page toolbar so it appears on every page.

title: Decide leftover regex rule '^Meet - -> Meeting' (keep or drop)
description: a303's test run left a panda regex rename rule (project ^Meet - -> Meeting) that merges 14 meeting projects into 'Meeting' on dashboards, non-destructively and reversibly. Keep it as the desired meeting-merge, or delete it for a clean slate.
close_reason: Decision: KEEP the ^Meet - -> Meeting regex rule — it's the desired non-destructive meeting-merge (reversible; raw preserved).
